children.label: A
children.text: Alaska Native organizations with experience operating programs that fulfill the purposes of this part;

children.label: i
children.text: a State educational agency or a local educational agency; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an Alaska Native organization that operates a program that fulfills the purposes of this part;

children.label: i
children.text: has experience operating programs that fulfill the purposes of this part; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is granted an official charter or sanction, as described in the definition of a tribal organization under , from at least one Alaska Native tribe or Alaska Native organization to carry out programs that meet the purposes of this part.section 5304 of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: The development and implementation of plans, methods, strategies, and activities to improve the educational outcomes of Alaska Natives.

children.label: B
children.text: The collection of data to assist in the evaluation of the programs carried out under this part.

children.label: i
children.text: Curriculum materials that are culturally informed and reflect the cultural diversity, languages, history, or the contributions of Alaska Native people, including curricula intended to preserve and promote Alaska Native culture.

children.label: ii
children.text: Instructional programs that make use of Alaska Native languages and cultures.

children.label: iii
children.text: Networks that develop, test, and disseminate best practices and introduce successful programs, materials, and techniques to meet the educational needs of Alaska Native students in urban and rural schools.

children.label: i
children.text: Pre-service and in-service training and professional development programs to prepare teachers to develop appreciation for, and understanding of, Alaska Native history, cultures, values, and ways of knowing and learning in order to effectively address the cultural diversity and unique needs of Alaska Native students and improve the teaching methods of educators.

children.label: ii
children.text: Recruitment and preparation of Alaska Native teachers.

children.label: iii
children.text: Programs that will lead to the certification and licensing of Alaska Native teachers, principals, other school leaders, and superintendents.

children.label: i
children.text: the development and operation of home visiting programs for Alaska Native preschool children, to ensure the active involvement of parents in their children’s education from the earliest ages;

children.label: ii
children.text: training, education, and support, including in-home visitation, for parents and caregivers of Alaska Native children to improve parenting and caregiving skills (including skills relating to discipline and cognitive development, reading readiness, observation, storytelling, and critical thinking);

children.label: iii
children.text: family literacy services;

children.label: iv
children.text: activities carried out under the Head Start Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: v
children.text: programs for parents and their infants, from the prenatal period of the infant through age 3;

children.label: vi
children.text: early childhood education programs; and

children.label: vii
children.text: native language immersion within early childhood education programs, Head Start, or preschool programs.

children.label: i
children.text: are designed to prepare Alaska Native students to excel in such subjects;

children.label: ii
children.text: provide appropriate support services to enable such students to benefit from the programs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: include activities that recognize and support the unique cultural and educational needs of Alaska Native children and incorporate appropriately qualified Alaska Native elders and other tradition bearers.

children.label: E
children.text: Research and data collection activities to determine the educational status and needs of Alaska Native children and adults and other such research and evaluation activities related to programs funded under this part.

children.label: i
children.text: remedial and enrichment programs;

children.label: I
children.text: programs of study and other instruction in Alaska Native history and ways of living to share the rich and diverse cultures of Alaska Natives among Alaska Native youth and elders, non-Native students and teachers, and the larger community;

children.label: II
children.text: instructing Alaska Native youth in leadership, communication, and Alaska Native culture, arts, history, and languages;

children.label: III
children.text: intergenerational learning and internship opportunities to Alaska Native youth and young adults;

children.label: IV
children.text: providing cultural immersion activities aimed at Alaska Native cultural preservation;

children.label: V
children.text: native language instruction and immersion activities, including native language immersion nests or schools;

children.label: VI
children.text: school-within-a-school model programs; and

children.label: VII
children.text: preparation for postsecondary education and career planning; and

children.label: I
children.text: address family instability and trauma; and

children.label: II
children.text: improve conditions for learning at home, in the community, and at school.

children.label: G
children.text: Student and teacher exchange programs, cross-cultural immersion programs, and culture camps designed to build mutual respect and understanding among participants.

children.label: H
children.text: Education programs for at-risk urban Alaska Native students that are designed to improve academic proficiency and graduation rates, use strategies otherwise permissible under this part, and incorporate a strong data collection and continuous evaluation component.

children.label: I
children.text: Strategies designed to increase the involvement of parents in their children’s education.

children.label: i
children.text: promote the academic progress and positive development of Alaska Native children and youth; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improve conditions for learning at home, in the community, and at school.

children.label: K
children.text: Career preparation activities to enable Alaska Native children and adults to prepare for meaningful employment, including programs providing mentoring, training, and apprenticeship activities.

children.label: L
children.text: Support for the development and operational activities of regional vocational schools in rural areas of Alaska to provide students with necessary resources to prepare for skilled employment opportunities.

children.label: M
children.text: Regional leadership academies that demonstrate effectiveness in building respect and understanding, and fostering a sense of Alaska Native identity in Alaska Native students to promote their pursuit of and success in completing higher education or career training.

children.label: N
children.text: Other activities, consistent with the purposes of this part, to meet the educational needs of Alaska Native children and adults.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: b Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $31,453,000 for each of fiscal years 2017 through 2020.
